validation_rules: require exported WorkItem, claim, dependency, transaction, receipt-drain, and closeout rows to remain projections and not live ledger authority
require scoped commit to remain the normal lane while broad checkpoint requires explicit operator authorization
require suspected secret or credential-equivalent material to hard-stop instead of entering a checkpoint lane
require copied Task Ledger, Work Ledger, and checkpoint lane source modules to match source-to-target digests and anchor checks

reject live Task Ledger mutation, live Work Ledger mutation, live Git mutation, private backup push execution, private-index mutation, provider calls, browser/HUD live access, account/session export, recipient work, publication, release, and whole-system correctness claims
require secret-exclusion scanning over mission bundle files and copied source modules with body text omitted from receipts
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite schedulability, claim preflight, scoped mutation, checkpoint lane decision, closeout projection, receipt drain, copied source manifests, source-module digest and anchor status, secret-exclusion scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

allowed_public_inputs: public WorkItem and claim projection rows
public scoped mutation and checkpoint lane policy rows
exact copied non-secret Task Ledger control modules
exact copied non-secret Work Ledger control modules
exact copied non-secret checkpoint lane modules
source refs, hashes, and anchor checks
synthetic negative cases around public mission transaction behavior

standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe mission transaction organ that validates work, claim, dependency, scoped commit, checkpoint lane, receipt-drain, and closeout projections while carrying exact copied non-secret Task Ledger, Work Ledger, and checkpoint lane source modules.
standard_payload.teleology: Give a cold reader the real control-plane body needed to inspect how Microcosm closes mission work without replacing live ledgers, live Git state, or private backup execution with receipt theater.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: A transaction receipt archive that describes Task Ledger, Work Ledger, or checkpoint behavior while omitting the public-safe source bodies and their digest, anchor, and secret-exclusion checks.
failure_modes: WorkItem or claim projection treated as live Task Ledger or Work Ledger authority
dirty tree treated as a blocker for scoped commits instead of a scoped-path discipline condition
broad checkpoint allowed without explicit operator authorization
suspected secret or credential-equivalent material enters a checkpoint lane instead of hard-stopping
claim preflight ignores same-path conflicts or stale expected parents
dependency unlock accepted without a resolution receipt
ready WorkItem scheduled despite unsatisfied hard dependencies
scoped commit receipt claims global authority
closeout projection drains unrelated receipts or omits exact drain scope
Task Ledger source module missing from the public bundle
Work Ledger source module missing from the public bundle
checkpoint lane source module missing from the public bundle
copied source module digest does not match macro source digest
copied source module lacks required ledger, transaction, checkpoint, rescue-ref, private-backup, or force-with-lease anchors
receipt duplicates full source body instead of refs, hashes, and verdicts
provider payload, browser/HUD live-access, account/session, credential, recipient-send, live ledger, or live Git state exported
public mission transaction pass claimed as release, private backup execution, source mutation, whole-system correctness, or live macro authority

anti_claim: This standard governs a public mission transaction runtime and exact copied non-secret control-plane source bodies. It does not authorize live Task Ledger mutation, live Work Ledger mutation, live Git mutation, private backup push execution, provider calls, browser/HUD access, account/session export, recipient work, publication, release, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness.
